title: Plugins
description: Official @hushkey/howl/plugins — Tailwind v4 and typed HTTP client generation

blocks.text: @hushkey/howl/plugins ships two official build-time plugins. tailwindPlugin wires Tailwind v4 into the CSS pipeline. httpClientGenPlugin crawls your *.api.ts files and emits a typed fetch client. Both plug into HowlBuilder and run inside the existing esbuild pipeline — no extra processes, no extra config files.

tailwindPlugin runs every .css file through PostCSS + @tailwindcss/postcss at build time. It registers itself as a static-file transform on the builder, so no CSS has to be authored differently — write Tailwind classes in your .tsx files and the plugin compiles utilities into your stylesheet on each build. Optimization (minify + tree-shake) is enabled automatically when the builder is in production mode.

Tailwind v4 has no JavaScript config requirement — utilities work zero-config. The tailwind.config.ts file is only needed if you want to extend the theme, change content globs, or add plugins like daisyUI.
Pass the builder instance to tailwindPlugin after constructing HowlBuilder. The plugin attaches to the default builder's static-file pipeline.

tailwindConfigBase is a re-exported Config literal with sensible defaults: content globs covering pages/, islands/, components/, layouts/; data-theme dark mode; and a bounce-once animation. Spread it in your project's tailwind.config.ts and override what you need.
TailwindPluginOptions also accepts base (override the injected base styles, or set null to skip) and optimize (boolean or { minify } — defaults to true in production).
In your entry CSS — usually static/style.css or client/style.css — import Tailwind v4 with the new @import syntax. The plugin handles the rest at build time.

httpClientGenPlugin crawls every *.api.ts file under apiDir, extracts the Zod params, requestBody, and 2xx response schemas, and emits a single TypeScript module that exports a tree-shaped http object. Each route becomes a $get/$post/... helper with full IntelliSense — call it from islands, the server, or a separate consumer package without hand-writing fetch wrappers.
The generator runs on every build start. Schemas referenced via imported consts are inlined into the output, so the generated client has no runtime dependency on your server code.
Pass httpClientGenPlugin via the builder's plugins array. It hooks esbuild's onStart, regenerating the client before each bundle.

aliases lets the generator follow imported schema identifiers across package boundaries. Example: { "@packages/": "../../packages/" } resolves import { UserSchema } from "@packages/shared/schemas.ts" while extracting requestBody dependencies.

The generated module exports an http object whose shape mirrors your /api tree. Path params, query, and body are all typed against the source defineApi() schemas. Pass origin: 'server' when calling from server-side code so DENO_API_URL is prefixed automatically.
The plugin also emits a validations object containing the raw Zod schemas for each route's requestBody, so consumers can validate locally before sending — useful for form libraries.
